description: A student-led protest movement in South Africa that began in October 2015 against a proposed increase in university tuition fees. The movement quickly expanded to demand the decolonization of education and free tertiary education, resulting in the government freezing fee hikes for 2016.

key_events.description: Students storm the South African Parliament during the mid-term budget speech.
President Jacob Zuma announces a zero percent fee increase for 2016.
Protests intensify at universities like Wits and UCT regarding historical debt.
High Court grants interdicts against student protesters leading to public discourse shift.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling student-led grassroots movements that experience rapid initial support before polarizing over tactics.
